requirements.part_name: Pivoting Vacuum Cup Glass Carrier - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Pivoting Vacuum Cup Glass Carrier'. The broader use
case is: Manual handling aid for lifting and positioning fragile glass panes in window shops or
installation sites. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Articulated yoke frame
holding suction cup mounts and a central hand grip. All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Allows an
operator to grip a glass sheet with multiple suction pads while the handle assembly pivots to suit
different carrying angles. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines
curved suction pads, hinge knuckles, offset handles, and repeated radial features that challenge
clear parametric articulation. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using
rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all
datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into
that same solid piece.

functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 185 mm x 40 mm x 60 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern

functional_features.name: functional center feature

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 46 mm x 18 mm x 29 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers

Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Glazing Bead Press-In Roller'. The broader use case
is: Shop-floor tool for seating flexible glazing beads or trim strips into window frame channels.
The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Forked roller head with a contoured wheel
and ergonomic handle socket. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be

while keeping the operator hand clear of the glass edge. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Requires modeling a small contoured wheel, fork clearance, handle transition,
and contact geometry without relying on simple block forms. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon
steel, normalized using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the
